227.reason: [When an error is detected (e.g., single '&' or '|'), the code still consumes only one character and continues. However, if the erroneous character is not a valid token and not handled by any case, it falls through to 'default' with flag=0, which skips adding to lexer but still consumes the character. But in the cases of '&' and '|', even though an error is recorded, the code proceeds to add a token because flag=14 or 15 (which are >0), incorrectly treating single '&' or '|' as valid tokens (AND/OR). This leads to both an error being recorded AND a token being output, violating the requirement that erroneous inputs should only produce error output.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [When an error is detected (e.g., single '&' or '|'), the code still consumes only one character and continues. But in the cases of '&' and '|', even though an error is recorded, the code proceeds to add a token because flag=14 or 15 (which are >0), incorrectly treating single '&' or '|' as valid tokens (AND/OR). This leads to both an error being recorded AND a token being output, violating the requirement that erroneous inputs should only produce error output.]

128.reason: [When a single '|' or '&' is encountered, an error should be reported, but the code still adds a token with flag=15 or flag=14 to lexer because flag>0. This incorrectly outputs a token instead of only reporting an error.]
